topic: GLM-5.1 vs Claude vs GPT-6: Open-Source Model That Beats Frontier Models

key_points: Multi-model comparison: GLM-5.1 vs Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek across coding and reasoning benchmarks
GLM-5.1 at 77.8% SWE-bench Verified — only 3 points behind Claude Opus 4.6 (80.8%) and GPT-5.2 (80.0%)
Pricing advantage: GLM-5.1 at $1.40/$4.40 per 1M tokens vs Claude Opus at $5/$25
Covers MIT license commercial use, self-hosting, and fine-tuning advantages
Full review: GLM-5.1 at 58.4 SWE-Bench Pro — #1 at launch on April 7, 2026
GLM-5.1 delivers 94.6% of Claude Opus 4.6 coding performance at 4.7x lower output cost
Code Arena Elo 1530 — first open-weight model to reach top 3 globally
8-hour autonomous coding sessions via Z.AI Slime async reinforcement learning framework
Direct benchmark comparison: Claude Opus 4.7 vs GLM-5.1 across SWE-bench, MCP Atlas, and coding tasks
Claude Opus 4.7 leads SWE-bench Pro at 64.3% vs GLM-5.1 at 58.4% — a 5.9-point margin
GLM-5.1 leads on price-performance: 3.5x cheaper input tokens for near-frontier coding quality
Covers context windows: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M tokens) vs GLM-5.1 (203K tokens)
GLM-5.1 matches GPT and Claude on coding benchmarks while being fully open-weight
Drop-in OpenAI SDK replacement via vLLM OpenAI-compatible API
MIT license allows fine-tuning for domain-specific coding agents
Comparison with GPT-5.4 ($6.60/M output) vs GLM-5.1 ($4.40/M output) — 33% cheaper
GLM-5.1 beat GPT-5.4 (57.7) and Claude Opus 4.6 (57.3) on SWE-Bench Pro at launch
Held #1 position for 9 days until Claude Opus 4.7 (64.3%) released on April 16, 2026
Still historically significant: first open-source model to claim #1 on real-repo software engineering benchmark
GPT-5.5 SWE-bench Pro score (58.6%) essentially ties GLM-5.1 at nearly 4x the cost

stats.fact: GLM-5.1 scored 58.4 on SWE-Bench Pro on April 7, 2026 — the #1 score globally at launch, beating GPT-5.4 (57.7) and Claude Opus 4.6 (57.3)
GLM-5.1 held #1 on SWE-Bench Pro for 9 days before Claude Opus 4.7 (64.3%) reclaimed the lead on April 16, 2026
GLM-5.1 Code Arena Elo: 1530 — #3 globally (behind claude-opus-4-6-thinking at 1548 and claude-opus-4-6 at 1542), first open-weight model in the top 3
GLM-5.1 SWE-bench Verified: 77.8% — 3 points behind Claude Opus 4.6 (80.8%) and GPT-5.2 (80.0%)
Claude Opus 4.7 (released April 16, 2026) leads SWE-bench Pro at 64.3%, SWE-bench Verified at 87.6%, and MCP Atlas at 79.1%
GPT-5.5 (released April 23, 2026) scores 58.6% SWE-bench Pro and 88.7% SWE-bench Verified — essentially tying GLM-5.1 on SWE-bench Pro at 4x the input cost
GLM-5.1 pricing: $1.40/M input, $4.40/M output — 3.5x cheaper on input and 5.7x cheaper on output than Claude Opus 4.7 ($5/$25)
GLM-5.1 delivers 94.6% of Claude Opus 4.6 coding performance at 7.8x lower output token cost
GPT-6 pre-training completed March 24, 2026 at Stargate (Abilene, TX); Polymarket odds 45% by June 30, 84% by Dec 31, 2026 for release
GPT-6 expected 40%+ performance gain over GPT-5.5 in coding, reasoning, and agentic tasks
GLM-5.1 self-hosting requires ~1,490GB VRAM at FP8 precision; 1-bit GGUF quantization (176GB) runs on ~180GB system RAM without GPU
GLM-5.1 autonomous mode completed 655 optimization iterations on a vector database, achieving 6.9x throughput improvement over 8-hour sessions
GPT-5.5 leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7% vs Claude Opus 4.7 (69.4%) — the benchmark most relevant for CLI and shell automation workflows
GLM-5.1 Code Arena Elo beats GPT-5.4 High (1457) by 73 points while being MIT-licensed and open-weight

angles: The 9-day reign at #1: GLM-5.1 was the first open-source model to top SWE-Bench Pro — how it happened and why it matters even after Claude Opus 4.7 reclaimed the crown
Price disruption that still holds: GLM-5.1 (58.4 SWE-bench Pro) vs GPT-5.5 (58.6) — essentially the same coding score at 3.5x lower input token cost
True open-source vs proprietary: MIT license means fine-tune, self-host, and commercialize — rights Claude and GPT-6 will never grant
GPT-6 preview: what a 40%+ improvement means for open-source competitiveness, and whether GLM-5.1's advantage erodes or deepens
8-hour autonomous coding sessions: GLM-5.1's agentic workload advantage over Claude task budgets and GPT-5.5
Code Arena milestone: first open-weight model in top 3 of human-evaluated agentic webdev — beating Gemini and GPT families by human preference
China open-source surge: GLM-5.1 from Z.AI follows DeepSeek in demonstrating that frontier-class AI is no longer exclusive to US labs
Self-hosting reality check: 1,490GB VRAM means 8xH200 GPUs — for teams who can afford it, GLM-5.1 eliminates per-token API costs entirely
